String.signature: public final class String implements Serializable, Comparable<String>, CharSequence
String.description: The String class represents character strings. All string literals in Java programs are implemented as instances of this class. Strings are immutable; their values cannot be changed after they are created.

length.description: Returns the length of this string. The length is equal to the number of Unicode code units in the string.

charAt.description: Returns the char value at the specified index. An index ranges from 0 to length() - 1.

substring.signature: public String substring(int beginIndex, int endIndex)
substring.description: Returns a string that is a substring of this string. The substring begins at beginIndex and extends to the character at endIndex - 1.

equals.description: Compares this string to the specified object. Returns true if the argument is a String object with the same character sequence.

equalsIgnoreCase.description: Compares this String to another String, ignoring case considerations.

compareTo.description: Compares two strings lexicographically. Returns a negative integer, zero, or positive integer.

contains.description: Returns true if this string contains the specified sequence of char values.

startsWith.description: Tests if this string starts with the specified prefix.

endsWith.description: Tests if this string ends with the specified suffix.

indexOf.description: Returns the index within this string of the first occurrence of the specified substring. Returns -1 if not found.

lastIndexOf.description: Returns the index within this string of the last occurrence of the specified substring. Returns -1 if not found.

replace.signature: public String replace(CharSequence target, CharSequence replacement)
replace.description: Replaces each substring of this string that matches the literal target sequence with the specified replacement sequence.

replaceAll.signature: public String replaceAll(String regex, String replacement)
replaceAll.description: Replaces each substring of this string that matches the given regular expression with the given replacement.

split.description: Splits this string around matches of the given regular expression.

trim.description: Returns a string whose value is this string, with all leading and trailing space removed, where space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020'.

strip.description: (Java 11+) Returns a string whose value is this string, with all leading and trailing white space removed. Uses Character.isWhitespace() for Unicode-aware trimming.

toUpperCase.description: Converts all of the characters in this String to upper case using the rules of the default locale.

toLowerCase.description: Converts all of the characters in this String to lower case using the rules of the default locale.

isEmpty.description: Returns true if, and only if, length() is 0.

isBlank.description: (Java 11+) Returns true if the string is empty or contains only white space codepoints.

toCharArray.description: Converts this string to a new character array.

matches.description: Tells whether or not this string matches the given regular expression.

format.signature: public static String format(String format, Object... args)
format.description: Returns a formatted string using the specified format string and arguments.

join.signature: public static String join(CharSequence delimiter, CharSequence... elements)
join.description: Returns a new String composed of copies of the CharSequence elements joined together with the specified delimiter.

valueOf.description: Returns the string representation of the Object argument. Returns "null" if the argument is null.

repeat.description: (Java 11+) Returns a string whose value is the concatenation of this string repeated count times.

chars.description: Returns an IntStream of char values from this sequence. The values are zero-extended to int.

ArrayList.signature: public class ArrayList<E> extends AbstractList<E> implements List<E>, RandomAccess, Cloneable, Serializable
ArrayList.description: Resizable-array implementation of the List interface. Implements all optional list operations and permits all elements, including null. Provides constant-time positional access and amortized constant-time addition.

add.description: Appends the specified element to the end of this list. Returns true.

get.description: Returns the element at the specified position in this list. Throws IndexOutOfBoundsException if the index is out of range.

set.signature: public E set(int index, E element)
set.description: Replaces the element at the specified position in this list with the specified element. Returns the element previously at the position.

remove.description: Removes the element at the specified position in this list. Shifts any subsequent elements to the left.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in this list.

isEmpty.description: Returns true if this list contains no elements.

contains.description: Returns true if this list contains the specified element, as determined by equals().

indexOf.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the specified element in this list, or -1 if not found.

clear.description: Removes all of the elements from this list. The list will be empty after this call returns.

sort.signature: public void sort(Comparator<? super E> c)
sort.description: Sorts this list according to the order induced by the specified Comparator. The sort is stable.

toArray.description: Returns an array containing all of the elements in this list in proper sequence (from first to last element).

subList.signature: public List<E> subList(int fromIndex, int toIndex)
subList.description: Returns a view of the portion of this list between the specified fromIndex (inclusive) and toIndex (exclusive).

iterator.description: Returns an iterator over the elements in this list in proper sequence.

forEach.signature: public void forEach(Consumer<? super E> action)
forEach.description: Performs the given action for each element of the Iterable until all elements have been processed or the action throws an exception.

stream.description: Returns a sequential Stream with this collection as its source.

addAll.signature: public boolean addAll(Collection<? extends E> c)
addAll.description: Appends all of the elements in the specified collection to the end of this list, in the order returned by the collection's iterator.

HashMap.signature: public class HashMap<K,V> extends AbstractMap<K,V> implements Map<K,V>, Cloneable, Serializable
HashMap.description: Hash table based implementation of the Map interface. This implementation permits null values and the null key. Makes no guarantees as to the order of the map. Provides constant-time performance for basic operations (get and put).

put.signature: public V put(K key, V value)
put.description: Associates the specified value with the specified key in this map. If the map previously contained a mapping for the key, the old value is replaced.

get.description: Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or null if this map contains no mapping for the key.

getOrDefault.signature: public V getOrDefault(Object key, V defaultValue)
getOrDefault.description: Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or defaultValue if this map contains no mapping for the key.

remove.description: Removes the mapping for the specified key from this map if present. Returns the previous value, or null.

containsKey.description: Returns true if this map contains a mapping for the specified key.

containsValue.description: Returns true if this map maps one or more keys to the specified value.

keySet.description: Returns a Set view of the keys contained in this map. The set is backed by the map.

values.description: Returns a Collection view of the values contained in this map. The collection is backed by the map.

entrySet.description: Returns a Set view of the mappings contained in this map. Each element is a Map.Entry.

size.description: Returns the number of key-value mappings in this map.

isEmpty.description: Returns true if this map contains no key-value mappings.

clear.description: Removes all of the mappings from this map. The map will be empty after this call returns.

putIfAbsent.signature: public V putIfAbsent(K key, V value)
putIfAbsent.description: If the specified key is not already associated with a value, associates it with the given value and returns null, else returns the current value.

computeIfAbsent.signature: public V computeIfAbsent(K key, Function<? super K, ? extends V> mappingFunction)
computeIfAbsent.description: If the specified key is not already associated with a value, attempts to compute its value using the given mapping function and enters it into this map.

merge.signature: public V merge(K key, V value, BiFunction<? super V, ? super V, ? extends V> remappingFunction)
merge.description: If the specified key is not already associated with a value, associates it with the given value. Otherwise, replaces the value with the results of the given remapping function.

forEach.signature: public void forEach(BiConsumer<? super K, ? super V> action)
forEach.description: Performs the given action for each entry in this map until all entries have been processed or the action throws an exception.

Optional.description: A container object which may or may not contain a non-null value. If a value is present, isPresent() returns true. If no value is present, the object is considered empty. Introduced in Java 8 to help avoid NullPointerException.

of.description: Returns an Optional describing the given non-null value. Throws NullPointerException if the value is null.

ofNullable.description: Returns an Optional describing the given value, or an empty Optional if the value is null.

empty.description: Returns an empty Optional instance. No value is present for this Optional.

isPresent.description: Returns true if a value is present, otherwise false.

isEmpty.description: (Java 11+) Returns true if a value is not present, otherwise false.

get.description: If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise throws NoSuchElementException. Prefer orElse/orElseThrow over get().

orElse.description: If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise returns other.

orElseGet.signature: public T orElseGet(Supplier<? extends T> supplier)
orElseGet.description: If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise invokes supplier and returns the result.

orElseThrow.signature: public <X extends Throwable> T orElseThrow(Supplier<? extends X> exceptionSupplier)
orElseThrow.description: If a value is present, returns the value, otherwise throws the exception created by the supplier.

map.signature: public <U> Optional<U> map(Function<? super T, ? extends U> mapper)
map.description: If a value is present, applies the provided mapping function to it, and if the result is non-null, returns an Optional describing the result.

flatMap.signature: public <U> Optional<U> flatMap(Function<? super T, ? extends Optional<? extends U>> mapper)
flatMap.description: If a value is present, applies the provided Optional-bearing mapping function to it and returns the result, otherwise returns an empty Optional.

filter.signature: public Optional<T> filter(Predicate<? super T> predicate)
filter.description: If a value is present and matches the given predicate, returns an Optional describing the value, otherwise returns an empty Optional.

ifPresent.signature: public void ifPresent(Consumer<? super T> action)
ifPresent.description: If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.

ifPresentOrElse.signature: public void ifPresentOrElse(Consumer<? super T> action, Runnable emptyAction)
ifPresentOrElse.description: (Java 9+) If a value is present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise performs the given empty-based action.

stream.description: (Java 9+) If a value is present, returns a sequential Stream containing only that value, otherwise returns an empty Stream.

Stream.signature: public interface Stream<T> extends BaseStream<T, Stream<T>>
Stream.description: A sequence of elements supporting sequential and parallel aggregate operations. Stream operations are divided into intermediate (lazy) and terminal (eager) operations. Streams are created from collections, arrays, or generator functions.

filter.description: Returns a stream consisting of the elements of this stream that match the given predicate. Intermediate operation.

map.signature: <R> Stream<R> map(Function<? super T, ? extends R> mapper)
map.description: Returns a stream consisting of the results of applying the given function to the elements of this stream. Intermediate operation.

flatMap.signature: <R> Stream<R> flatMap(Function<? super T, ? extends Stream<? extends R>> mapper)
flatMap.description: Returns a stream of the results of replacing each element with the contents of a mapped stream. Intermediate operation.

collect.signature: <R, A> R collect(Collector<? super T, A, R> collector)
collect.description: Performs a mutable reduction operation using a Collector. Terminal operation.

reduce.description: Performs a reduction on the elements using the given identity value and an associative accumulation function. Terminal operation.

forEach.description: Performs an action for each element of this stream. Terminal operation.

sorted.description: Returns a stream consisting of the elements of this stream, sorted according to natural order. Stateful intermediate operation.

distinct.description: Returns a stream consisting of the distinct elements (according to Object.equals) of this stream. Stateful intermediate operation.

limit.description: Returns a stream consisting of the elements of this stream, truncated to be no longer than maxSize in length. Short-circuiting stateful intermediate operation.

skip.description: Returns a stream consisting of the remaining elements of this stream after discarding the first n elements. Stateful intermediate operation.

count.description: Returns the count of elements in this stream. Terminal operation.

findFirst.description: Returns an Optional describing the first element of this stream, or an empty Optional if the stream is empty. Short-circuiting terminal operation.

findAny.description: Returns an Optional describing some element of the stream, or an empty Optional if the stream is empty. Non-deterministic in parallel streams. Short-circuiting terminal operation.

anyMatch.description: Returns whether any elements of this stream match the provided predicate. Short-circuiting terminal operation.

allMatch.description: Returns whether all elements of this stream match the provided predicate. Short-circuiting terminal operation.

noneMatch.description: Returns whether no elements of this stream match the provided predicate. Short-circuiting terminal operation.

toArray.description: Returns an array containing the elements of this stream. Terminal operation.

peek.description: Returns a stream consisting of the elements of this stream, additionally performing the provided action on each element as they are consumed. Intermediate operation mainly for debugging.

min.description: Returns the minimum element of this stream according to the provided Comparator. Terminal operation.

max.description: Returns the maximum element of this stream according to the provided Comparator. Terminal operation.

of.description: Returns a sequential ordered stream whose elements are the specified values.

iterate.signature: static <T> Stream<T> iterate(T seed, UnaryOperator<T> f)
iterate.description: Returns an infinite sequential ordered Stream produced by iterative application of a function f to an initial element seed.

generate.signature: static <T> Stream<T> generate(Supplier<? extends T> s)
generate.description: Returns an infinite sequential unordered stream where each element is generated by the provided Supplier.

concat.signature: static <T> Stream<T> concat(Stream<? extends T> a, Stream<? extends T> b)
concat.description: Creates a lazily concatenated stream whose elements are all the elements of the first stream followed by all the elements of the second stream.

List.description: An ordered collection (also known as a sequence). The user can access elements by their integer index and search for elements in the list. Unlike sets, lists typically allow duplicate elements.

of.description: (Java 9+) Returns an unmodifiable list containing the specified elements. Throws NullPointerException if any element is null.

copyOf.signature: static <E> List<E> copyOf(Collection<? extends E> coll)
copyOf.description: (Java 10+) Returns an unmodifiable List containing the elements of the given Collection, in its iteration order.

Map.description: An object that maps keys to values. A map cannot contain duplicate keys; each key can map to at most one value. This interface takes the place of the Dictionary class.

of.signature: static <K,V> Map<K,V> of(K k1, V v1, ...)
of.description: (Java 9+) Returns an unmodifiable map containing the specified key-value pairs. Does not permit null keys or values.

ofEntries.signature: static <K,V> Map<K,V> ofEntries(Map.Entry<? extends K, ? extends V>... entries)
ofEntries.description: (Java 9+) Returns an unmodifiable map containing keys and values extracted from the given entries. Does not permit duplicate keys.

entry.signature: static <K,V> Map.Entry<K,V> entry(K k, V v)
entry.description: (Java 9+) Returns an unmodifiable Map.Entry containing the given key and value. These entries are suitable for populating Map instances using the Map.ofEntries() method.

CompletableFuture.signature: public class CompletableFuture<T> implements Future<T>, CompletionStage<T>
CompletableFuture.description: A Future that may be explicitly completed (setting its value and status), and may be used as a CompletionStage, supporting dependent functions and actions that trigger upon its completion. Provides a rich API for composing asynchronous operations.

supplyAsync.description: Returns a new CompletableFuture that is asynchronously completed by a task running in the ForkJoinPool.commonPool() with the value obtained by calling the given Supplier.

thenApply.signature: public <U> CompletableFuture<U> thenApply(Function<? super T, ? extends U> fn)
thenApply.description: Returns a new CompletionStage that, when this stage completes normally, is executed with this stage's result as the argument to the supplied function.

thenAccept.signature: public CompletableFuture<Void> thenAccept(Consumer<? super T> action)
thenAccept.description: Returns a new CompletionStage that, when this stage completes normally, is executed with this stage's result as the argument to the supplied action.

thenRun.description: Returns a new CompletionStage that, when this stage completes normally, executes the given action. The result is not passed to the action.

thenCompose.signature: public <U> CompletableFuture<U> thenCompose(Function<? super T, ? extends CompletionStage<U>> fn)
thenCompose.description: Returns a new CompletionStage that is completed with the same value as the CompletionStage returned by the given function. Equivalent to flatMap for futures.

thenCombine.signature: public <U,V> CompletableFuture<V> thenCombine(CompletionStage<? extends U> other, BiFunction<? super T, ? super U, ? extends V> fn)
thenCombine.description: Returns a new CompletionStage that, when this and the other given stage both complete normally, is executed with the two results as arguments to the supplied function.

exceptionally.signature: public CompletableFuture<T> exceptionally(Function<Throwable, ? extends T> fn)
exceptionally.description: Returns a new CompletableFuture that, when this stage completes exceptionally, is executed with the exception as the argument to the supplied function.

join.description: Returns the result value when complete, or throws an (unchecked) exception if completed exceptionally. Unlike get(), does not throw checked exceptions.

get.signature: public T get() throws InterruptedException, ExecutionException
get.description: Waits if necessary for this future to complete, and then returns its result. Throws ExecutionException if the computation threw an exception.

allOf.description: Returns a new CompletableFuture that is completed when all of the given CompletableFutures complete.

anyOf.description: Returns a new CompletableFuture that is completed when any of the given CompletableFutures complete, with the same result.

completedFuture.description: Returns a new CompletableFuture that is already completed with the given value.

Math.description: Contains methods for performing basic numeric operations such as exponentiation, logarithm, square root, and trigonometry.

max.signature: static int max(int a, int b)
max.description: Returns the greater of two values.

min.signature: static int min(int a, int b)
min.description: Returns the smaller of two values.

pow.signature: static double pow(double a, double b)
pow.description: Returns the value of the first argument raised to the power of the second argument.

sqrt.description: Returns the correctly rounded positive square root.

random.description: Returns a pseudorandom double value between 0.0 and 1.0.

ceil.description: Returns the smallest double that is greater than or equal to the argument and is an integer.

floor.description: Returns the largest double that is less than or equal to the argument and is an integer.

round.description: Returns the closest long to the argument.

PI.description: The double value closer to pi than any other.

StringBuilder.signature: public final class StringBuilder extends AbstractStringBuilder
StringBuilder.description: A mutable sequence of characters. Unlike String, StringBuilder can be modified after creation.

insert.description: Inserts the string at the specified offset.

replace.signature: StringBuilder replace(int start, int end, String str)

charAt.description: Returns the char value at the specified index.

Arrays.description: Contains static methods for manipulating arrays (sorting, searching, comparing, filling).

sort.description: Sorts the specified array into ascending numerical order.

binarySearch.signature: static int binarySearch(int[] a, int key)
binarySearch.description: Searches the specified sorted array for the specified value using binary search.

copyOf.signature: static int[] copyOf(int[] original, int newLength)
copyOf.description: Copies the specified array, truncating or padding with zeros as necessary.

asList.description: Returns a fixed-size list backed by the specified array.

fill.signature: static void fill(int[] a, int val)
fill.description: Assigns the specified value to each element.

equals.signature: static boolean equals(int[] a, int[] a2)
equals.description: Returns true if the two arrays are equal.

stream.description: Returns a sequential IntStream with the specified array as source.

toString.description: Returns a string representation of the contents.

Collections.description: Consists exclusively of static methods that operate on or return collections.

sort.signature: static <T extends Comparable<? super T>> void sort(List<T> list)
sort.description: Sorts the specified list into ascending order.

min.signature: static <T extends Object & Comparable<? super T>> T min(Collection<? extends T> coll)

max.signature: static <T extends Object & Comparable<? super T>> T max(Collection<? extends T> coll)

unmodifiableList.signature: static <T> List<T> unmodifiableList(List<? extends T> list)

frequency.signature: static int frequency(Collection<?> c, Object o)
frequency.description: Returns the number of elements equal to the specified object.

singletonList.description: Returns an immutable list containing only the specified object.

File.signature: public class File implements Serializable, Comparable<File>
File.description: An abstract representation of file and directory pathnames.

exists.description: Tests whether the file or directory denoted by this pathname exists.

getName.description: Returns the name of the file or directory.

getPath.description: Converts this pathname into a pathname string.

isFile.description: Tests whether the file denoted by this pathname is a normal file.

isDirectory.description: Tests whether it denotes a directory.

length.description: Returns the length of the file in bytes.

listFiles.description: Returns an array of pathnames denoting the files in the directory.

createNewFile.description: Atomically creates a new empty file named by this pathname.

Thread.description: A thread is a thread of execution in a program. The JVM allows an application to have multiple threads running concurrently.

start.description: Causes this thread to begin execution.

run.description: The entry point for the thread.

sleep.description: Causes the currently executing thread to sleep.

join.description: Waits for this thread to die.

isAlive.description: Tests if this thread is alive.

currentThread.description: Returns a reference to the currently executing thread.

Scanner.signature: public final class Scanner implements Iterator<String>, Closeable
Scanner.description: A simple text scanner which can parse primitive types and strings using regular expressions.

nextLine.description: Advances past the current line and returns the input that was skipped.

nextInt.description: Scans the next token as an int.

nextDouble.description: Scans the next token as a double.

hasNext.description: Returns true if there is another token in input.

hasNextLine.description: Returns true if there is another line in input.

Integer.signature: public final class Integer extends Number implements Comparable<Integer>
Integer.description: Wraps a value of the primitive type int. Provides methods for converting between int and String.

parseInt.description: Parses the string argument as a signed decimal integer.

valueOf.description: Returns an Integer instance representing the specified int.

toString.description: Returns a String object representing the specified integer.

MAX_VALUE.description: The maximum value an int can have, 2^31-1.

MIN_VALUE.description: The minimum value an int can have, -2^31.

intValue.description: Returns the value of this Integer as an int.

toBinaryString.description: Returns a string representation of the integer in base 2.
